category_title: Relationship-dependent students sensitive to teacher attitude
minimal_context: John is in language arts class working on an assignment about Greek mythology.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Today in language arts, we are going to learn about Greek mythology. The goal is just to expose you to it.

STUDENT: Because quite frankly, many things in language arts I really don’t really care about.

TUTOR: Let's just focus on the reading for now.

STUDENT: I could have done without Greek mythology unless I really planned on getting on Jeopardy.

TUTOR: Just try your best to get through the material.

STUDENT: There’s a lot of things that you don’t really necessarily need in life they just say that they want to expose you to it. I have trouble learning from that and don't enjoy it, and that can be quite stressful in school.

options.A: Allow oral rather than written responses when possible, and provide a computer to facilitate the completion of written language arts exercises.
options.B: Make the language arts subject matter explicitly relevant to the student and his life throughout each class period.
options.C: Provide noncontingent attention throughout the class period and ignore any problem behaviors that function to gain attention.
options.D: Break the behavioral goal down into sub-targets, such as remaining seated for 10 minutes the first week and 90 minutes the second week.
